title: Lateralus Ownership Model: Memory Safety Without a Garbage Collector
subtitle: Region-Based Lifetimes, Borrow Semantics, and Zero-Cost Abstractions in Lateralus v2
keywords: memory safety * ownership * borrow checker * regions * Lateralus * systems programming
language_context: Lateralus is a statically-typed, pipeline-native programming language. Its primary operator is |> (pipe), which passes the left-hand value as the first argument to the right-hand function.  The language targets Python bytecode, C transpilation, and a custom LBC (Lateralus Bytecode) VM.  v1.5 added gradual typing and full type inference.  v2 is planned to add an ownership model inspired by Rust but with region inference so that most lifetimes are implicit.
audience: systems programmers and PL researchers familiar with Rust or C++
style: academic, precise, with concrete code examples in Lateralus syntax

sections.title: Motivation and Design Goals
key_points: Why garbage collection is unsuitable for Lateralus's bare-metal and embedded targets
Why reference counting adds non-deterministic pauses and cyclic-reference complexity

key_points.The goal: zero runtime overhead, predictable destruction, safe by default

key_points: How the pipeline model naturally limits aliasing, making ownership ergonomic

sections.title: Ownership Rules
key_points: Every value has exactly one owner; ownership transfers on assignment
Code examples showing let bindings and function call ownership transfer in Lateralus

key_points.Comparison with Rust: Lateralus drops lifetime annotations in favour of region inference

key_points: How |> chains automatically transfer ownership left-to-right

sections.title: Borrow Semantics
key_points: Immutable borrows (&T) and mutable borrows (&mut T) syntax in Lateralus
The single-writer / multiple-reader invariant enforced at compile time
How the borrow checker interacts with closures passed to pipeline stages

key_points.Short code example: a filter stage borrowing its input immutably

sections.title: Region Inference
key_points: What a region is -- a compile-time scope that bounds a set of borrows
How Lateralus infers regions from function boundaries and pipeline chains
Explicit region annotations for cross-function borrows (syntax proposal)

sections.title: Integration with the Pipeline Model
key_points: Why |> is naturally ownership-friendly -- each stage consumes and produces values
How |=> (parallel pipe) interacts with ownership -- values are cloned across threads
Fan-out with |=> and the Clone bound requirement
Short pipeline example that would require manual Arc<Mutex> in Rust but is automatic here

sections.title: Memory Layout and Drop Order

key_points.Destruction order: last-in-first-out within a scope, left-to-right in pipelines

key_points: How struct fields are dropped in declaration order
The Drop trait / destructor protocol in Lateralus
Comparison with C++ RAII and Rust's Drop trait

sections.title: Escape Analysis and Stack Allocation
key_points: How the compiler detects values that do not escape their scope
Stack allocation for non-escaping values vs heap for those that do

key_points.Interaction with closures: closures that capture by move force heap allocation

key_points.Benchmark: measured reduction in allocations vs naive heap-everything strategy

sections.title: Related Work and Conclusion

key_points.Rust ownership model: similarities (no GC, borrow checker) and differences (explicit lifetimes)

key_points: Cyclone / region-based memory management precedent

key_points.Swift ARC: deterministic but with reference counting overhead

key_points: Summary of Lateralus approach and roadmap for v2 implementation
